request: Design a Premium-tier launch page for revüe v1.1's own tier system, through the full v1.1 pipeline (tier -> elevate -> conformance -> brand).

inputs.note: the Premium craft bar this page must itself clear
the flagship reference for what Premium looks like

brief.deliverable: Single-file HTML Premium launch one-pager, desktop + mobile + sticky action bar

forbiddenElements.why: brief specifies no bespoke masthead — the hero opens the page

hardNos.why: brand voice: never generic link text

options.summary: Cinematic tier ladder: full-bleed gradient hero, Fraunces display headlines, tiers as an editorial sequence, sticky notify bar.
Gate-transcript: the page shows its own audit output as the proof section, terminal-styled band inside the Premium shell.
Split flagship-vs-template comparison hero, the two bars shown side by side.

optionFeedback.note: the ladder reads Premium on its own; keep as the spine
keep one line of the self-audit idea in the proof section copy, drop the terminal styling
comparison splits are the generic-marketing trope the anti-generic bar exists to kill

evidence.statement: Read docs/brand.md, references/elevate.md, and examples/premium-exemplar.html this session before the brief; the lock adds Fraunces to the revüe palette because Premium requires a serif display face.

Ran scripts/validate-output.py --tier premium on winner-draft-1.html this session: base audit clean, elevatePass false, all four elevate checks failing — the clean-template-declared-Premium failure, caught by the tier gate alone (conformance and brand both passed it).
Ran scripts/validate-conformance.py on winner.html this session: 4 sections in order, no forbidden masthead, [LAUNCH_DATE] visibly present; exit code 0. Object attached verbatim.

Ran scripts/validate-output.py --tier premium on winner.html this session: 4 colors all in lock, Fraunces+Quicksand pairing detected, all four elevate checks pass; exit code 0. Audit attached verbatim.

Elevate rules 3-5 (editorial restraint, signature motif, scrim sufficiency) are judgment calls not covered by the script; assessed by self-check only, no rendered-browser screenshot in this environment.

scorecard.evidence: 640px breakpoint; hero and sections collapse cleanly
Static review only — no rendered-browser screenshot in this environment
Output audit: all colors and fonts in lock; elevatePass true
File path shared; opens in any browser
Single file, sectioned CSS, comments map each elevate rule to its implementation

elevateChecks.detail: hero section with full-bleed imagery detected (imagery + cover/vh in scope)
scrim/overlay/gradient/text-shadow detected in the hero's own scope

elevateChecks.check: display type system (serif display + sans body)
elevateChecks.detail: serif display + sans body pairing detected (display: 'Fraunces' on 'h1, h2'; body: 'Quicksand')
bottom-anchored sticky/fixed bar '.sticky-bar' contains a CTA link/button

verdict.reason: The brief declared Premium and the tier gate proved it matters: draft 1 was clean, on-brand, structurally conforming — and failed all four elevate checks. The rebuilt winner passes the brand audit, the Premium bar, and the conformance check. One bounded change remains: render in a real browser and confirm both breakpoints and the scrim's visual sufficiency before publishing.
verdict.requiredProof: outputAudit.pass + elevatePass + conformance.pass (all attached) + design scorecard.
verdict.nextAction: Open examples/dogfood-premium/winner.html in a browser, confirm breakpoints and scrim, then publish.

pathToShip.item: Open winner.html in a real browser; confirm desktop + mobile rendering and that the hero scrim is visually sufficient (elevate rule 5 is a judgment call)
